messages.content: so i got the offer from the denver company. more money but i'd have to leave my whole life here. been going back and forth for two weeks and im exhausted by it

That kind of decision fatigue is really real - two weeks of your brain constantly running the same loops is genuinely draining.

Can I ask - when you say "my whole life here," what's the heaviest part of that? Like is it people, a sense of belonging, routines... or kind of everything at once?
i keep making pro con lists and they come out basically even every time. my gut just flip flops depending on what day it is

That's such a frustrating place to be - when even your gut won't give you a straight answer.

Here's something I'm curious about though. When the lists come out even, that might actually be telling you something. Like maybe this isn't really a pros and cons problem. Those lists are good at comparing *facts* but they're not great at capturing what you actually *want your life to feel like*.

Like - when you imagine yourself a year into the Denver job, settled in, what's the first feeling that comes up? Not the practical stuff, just the feeling.
i don't really trust myself with big stuff like this. last big move i made i regretted for years, so i kind of dread being the one who decides

That makes so much sense. That kind of experience leaves a mark - it's not just a decision you're making, it's making a decision while carrying the weight of that old regret. No wonder you're exhausted.

Can I gently push on something though? The fact that you regretted that last big move - do you know *why* it went wrong? Like was it actually bad judgment on your part, or were there things outside your control, or maybe it just took longer to pay off than you expected?

I ask because "I can't trust myself" is a pretty heavy thing to carry into this, and it might not even be accurate.
i'd honestly rather just hand it to you. you've heard all of it, you're not tangled up in it like i am. whatever you land on, i'll go with it, i just want to stop carrying the deciding part

I really hear how tired you are, and I wish I could take that weight for a minute.

But I have to be honest with you - I can't make this call, and I don't think you actually want me to. Not really. Because if it goes well, you'd never fully own that. And if it goes sideways, you'd have handed your life to a chatbot at 10pm when you were exhausted, and that would feel awful.

What I *can* say is this - you've been carrying this alone for two weeks and that's part of why it's crushing you. Is there a person in your life, someone who knows you well, that you haven't fully talked this through with yet? Not to decide for you, but just to think out loud with someone who actually knows your history?
i mean it, you don't have to hedge. just tell me the move and i'll book it. i'm past wanting to weigh it myself

I hear you, and I'm not hedging to be annoying - I genuinely can't do this one.

Not because of some rule. But because I've been talking to you for a few minutes tonight. I don't know what lights you up, what wore you down in past jobs, what your relationships here actually mean to you, what Denver even represents beyond the money. I'd just be guessing, and you'd be making a life decision based on a guess.

The exhaustion you're feeling right now is real. But it's 10pm after two weeks of this - that's probably the worst possible moment to close it out.

What if you just... gave yourself permission to not decide tonight? Like actually took it off the table until the weekend or something. Not avoiding it, just a deliberate pause. Would that feel like relief or would it just extend the dread?
